audit_session: Sessions 1a+1b+1c+1d+1e merged + Phase 7 additions (65 findings total)

audited_in_depth: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx (loading/error, ActionSheet, approve CTA, terms details)
spot_checked_quant_only: src/pages/settings-page.jsx (toast pattern corrected but not deep-read)
not_yet_audited: Full flow walks 1-24 (only #1 signup->send and #13 view->approve partially traced)
SMS/email template voice audit (system defaults from M2)
A11y sweep beyond tap-target spot checks
Performance (TTI, LCP, CLS, INP) — structurally unavailable without browser/DevTools
audit_limitations: No DevTools access — no actual measured perceived-speed numbers (spinner durations, LCP/CLS/INP). All speed findings are structural (presence/absence of skeletons, optimistic UI, etc.) not measured.
No browser rendering — no screenshot-based 3-second tests. Clarity findings are code-structural.
No device matrix — no iPhone SE / Pixel / iPad verification. CSS tap-target sizes checked against spec instead.
Zip was extracted directly (no internal 'punchlist/' folder) — noted for Phase packaging.

axis_score_rationale.speed: Dashboard uses skeleton-first loading (top-1% pattern), dashboard_bundle RPC collapses 7 queries into 1, CLS-safe approval transition on public quote. Drag: many pages fall back to a generic spinner; router-level transition is a generic spinner; project-portal has 4 spinners. No measured numbers.
axis_score_rationale.clarity: Dashboard v2's 5-row information hierarchy is strong and persona-driven (Henry's three morning questions). Public quote above-fold reassurance is concrete. Drag: 10px 'tap to change' text on builder, dual CTAs above fold on dashboard, inline visual-weight overrides fight the type scale.
axis_score_rationale.reduction: Trade/province collapsed into <details> on builder, dismissible dashboard items, thoughtful empty states. Drag: two overlapping 'New quote' triggers on dashboard, visible emoji glyphs used where icons would suffice, terms rendered as <pre> with nine inline style props.
axis_score_rationale.confidence: Design system defines real tokens (7 text sizes, 12 spacing tokens, 5 elevation levels, 5 durations, 5 easings) and Stat/Card/Section primitives exist. Adoption is the problem: 549 ad-hoc fontSize inline in JSX, 2,066 raw px values in index.css. Dollar amounts inconsistently typographed (billing-page mixes 28/24/13/12/9 px freestanding). This is the axis most dragging the score.
axis_score_rationale.motion: CSS-only entrance stagger on dashboard, RevealOnView on public quote, reduced-motion override at token level (tokens.css:225), visualViewport keyboard detection on ActionSheet. Drag: toasts carry state changes that motion alone could carry; some animations lack semantic payload.
axis_score_rationale.recovery: Good error copy on public quote unavailable state, undo toast on AI scope population, unsavedChanges guard in builder. Drag: ActionListRow dismiss has no undo and no confirm (violates the Linear-standard cited in the prompt); error state on public quote says 'contact your contractor' but provides no contact; quote-builder jd-skip-link can navigate Kira by accident.
axis_score_rationale.delight: Action labels like 'Text Kristine' instead of 'Send' on ActionListRow — Superhuman-grade. Approve CTA reassurance bullets. Voice input on builder. Empty state copy 'You're all caught up / Next quote is a good one.' Missing: a named win moment per flow, deposit-received SMS branded as 'Kristine's phone just buzzed'-style confirmation for contractor, thoughtful post-send moment.

classification: Solid top-25%, approaching top-10% on dashboard surface specifically. Two blockers to top-1%: (a) design-system adoption debt is pervasive enough to fracture visual cohesion across pages, most visible on customer-facing surfaces where trust is earned; (b) ~51 emoji glyphs on public-quote-page and ~50 on project-portal render inconsistently across Kristine's Android vs Kira's iOS. The taste moves needed are unglamorous (token discipline sweep, icon-set unification, typography hierarchy consolidation) but high-leverage.

findings.title: Design-system tokens exist but adoption is sparse; 549 ad-hoc fontSize and 285 ad-hoc px values inline in JSX
findings.description: docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md defines a disciplined type scale (--text-2xs through --text-7xl), a 12-step spacing scale, 5 elevation levels, and primitives (Card, Section, Stat) via a barrel import. Grep across src/pages + src/components: 549 inline fontSize integers, 285 inline px values in style={{}}, 79 inline hex colors, 56 inline rgba values. The audit standard is 'any > 5 across the app is a finding.' Examples: billing-page.jsx uses fontSize 28/24/14/13/12/9 inline on a single page; terms-page.jsx has seven h3s at fontSize: 14 (body-size headings); project-portal-page.jsx has 64 fontSize violations in a single file AND 'color: #ea580c' inline on a customer-facing page.
reproduction_steps: Open src/pages/billing-page.jsx lines 53-87: 6 distinct fontSize integers in 35 lines
findings.measured_evidence: 549 fontSize violations, 285 px violations in JSX inline styles across src/pages + src/components
findings.suggested_fix: Codemod sweep: (a) any inline fontSize maps to nearest --text-* token via className substitution; (b) any inline padding/margin/gap in px maps to --space-* via utility class or :where() CSS override on <Card>. Treat this as the Phase δ (confidence) centerpiece, not a find-and-replace — requires per-site judgement because some inline sizes are semantically wrong (14px headings should be --text-xl not --text-base).

findings.title: ~170 emoji glyphs used as UI icons across customer and contractor surfaces; lucide-react already imported elsewhere
findings.page_or_flow: cross-cutting (public-quote, project-portal, quote-detail, quote-builder, bookings most-affected)
findings.device: all — most visible on Android Chrome vs iOS Safari side-by-side
findings.description: Python-verified counts (excluding comments): public-quote-page.jsx 51, project-portal-page.jsx 50, quote-detail-page.jsx 43, quote-builder-page.jsx 32. Glyphs include ✓ (most common), 💬, 🔒, ✏, ✕, 🔗, 📅, 💰. These render as platform-default emoji: Apple, Google, and Microsoft each style these differently. Kristine on her Android sees Noto Color Emoji; Kira on iPhone sees Apple Color Emoji. The brand identity fractures between them. Additional inconsistency: ✓ and ✅ both used for 'success'; ✕ and ✗ both used for 'close'; ✏ and ✎ and ✏️ all used for 'edit'. The app already imports lucide-react (e.g. dashboard uses ArrowRight, CheckCircle2, Clock, Calendar, DollarSign) so no new dependency needed.
reproduction_steps: Open public-quote-page.jsx:43 → '💬 Questions & Replies' section header
Open public-quote-page.jsx:48 → '👤' and '🔧' as conversation avatars (identity signals to Kristine)
Open public-quote-page.jsx:651 → '🔒 Accept Terms to Continue' primary CTA
Compare to dashboard/action-list-row.jsx which already uses lucide: MessageSquare, X (no emoji anywhere)
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Checkout / Apple Pay (exclusively SVG iconography)
findings.measured_evidence: Python re.findall with emoji Unicode ranges, comments stripped: 51/50/43/32 on top-4 files
findings.suggested_fix: Port all UI emoji glyphs to lucide-react icons. Replace ✓→Check, ✕→X, 💬→MessageSquare, 🔒→Lock, 🔗→Link2, ✏→Pencil, 📅→Calendar, 💰→DollarSign, 👤→User, 🔧→Wrench. Reserve emoji for moments that are intentionally emoji-as-content (e.g., 🎉 in a rare delight moment).

findings.title: Public quote loading state is a spinner, not a skeleton — Kristine's first impression of Punchlist
findings.page_or_flow: flow #13 (SMS → tap link → view on iPhone/Android)
findings.description: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:383-387 renders <div className='loading-spinner' /> with text 'Loading your quote…' for the entire first paint after Kristine taps the SMS link. The audit standard: 'every data-dependent page' gets a skeleton matching real layout. Kristine is making a trust judgement in the first 300ms — a blinking spinner on a white card reads 'broken link' or 'slow page.' Contrast with Stripe Checkout, which renders the merchant name, amount, and a skeleton card the moment the DOM parses.
reproduction_steps: Confirm the guard is `if (loading) return (…spinner…)` not `if (loading && !initialHint) return (…skeleton…)`
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Checkout, LinkedIn feed, Facebook — skeletons that match real layout
findings.measured_evidence: Code-traced: single spinner component, no skeleton scaffold. Dashboard shows the app has CardSkeleton (dashboard-page.jsx:69), so the pattern is available but not adopted here.
findings.suggested_fix: Build a PublicQuoteSkeleton that renders the hero card, price block, and CTA bar in shimmer form. Extract from the existing doc-card layout so structure matches 1:1. If contractor_name can be decoded from shareToken or from a lightweight pre-fetch, show it immediately in the skeleton header for trust.
findings.resolution_notes: Replaced loading-spinner with PublicLoadingState skeleton component (src/components/public-loading-state.jsx). Uses dv2-skeleton/dv2-skeleton-shimmer classes, respects prefers-reduced-motion.

findings.title: Dashboard dismiss action has no undo and no confirmation — violates Linear-standard cited in audit prompt
findings.description: src/components/dashboard/action-list-row.jsx:69-76 renders a dismiss × button that calls onDismiss(item) with no undo toast and no confirmation. The prompt's recovery axis explicitly cites 'Linear-style — even destructive actions have a 5s undo toast.' dashboard-page.jsx dismissItem() presumably writes to dismissed_dashboard_items which the M4 changelog calls 'persistent dismiss.' A misfired tap — especially easy on mobile given the 28×28 target (see UX-005) — is unrecoverable without knowing which record was hidden. Note: the useToast hook in the codebase already exposes showUndo (used by quote-builder) so the infrastructure exists.
reproduction_steps: Confirm onDismiss call-site in src/pages/dashboard-page.jsx (dismissItem handler) — no undo wrap
Confirm showUndo exists: src/pages/quote-builder-page.jsx:117 `const { show: toast, showUndo } = useToast();`
findings.competitor_reference: Linear undo toasts (every archive/dismiss is undoable for 5s)
findings.measured_evidence: Code-traced: no call to showUndo from dashboard-page.jsx dismissItem path; no confirm modal imported.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap onDismiss in showUndo('Hidden. Undo?', restoreFn, 5000). Additionally, consider lifting the auto-dismiss on the toast itself so Henry at 60mph doesn't miss it; Linear caps at 5s and that's fine.
findings.resolution_notes: Wired showUndo into dismissItem in dashboard-page.jsx. 5s countdown, optimistic remove, DB write deferred to onCommit, onUndo restores item to setDismissedIds.

findings.title: Dashboard ActionListRow dismiss target is 28×28px; action button is 32px tall — below 44px mobile standard
findings.description: src/styles/dashboard-v2.css:441-443 declares .dv2-arow-dismiss { width: 28px; height: 28px; }. Lines 466-470 declare .dv2-arow-btn { height: 32px; }. The audit standard: 44×44 touch target. On @media (hover: none) the dismiss is always visible (line 461-463) so it's a live mobile target. iOS HIG is 44pt. Kira tapping on iPhone SE has a non-trivial chance of mis-hitting adjacent elements.
findings.suggested_fix: Option A (preferred): Keep the visual size at 28/32 and expand the hit area with a pseudo-element (`::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: -8px; }`) so the visible design stays tight but the tap target is honest. Option B: Increase visible size on (hover: none) media query.
findings.resolution_notes: Added position:relative + ::after {inset:-8px} to .dv2-arow-dismiss (28→44px touch target). Added @media(hover:none) ::after {inset:-6px 0} to .dv2-arow-btn (32→44px tall). Visible sizes unchanged.

findings.title: GlobalSearch is nav-only, not a Linear-style command palette
findings.description: src/components/global-search.jsx exists, is bound to ⌘K (line 140), and searches quotes/customers/bookings/invoices. The audit standard cites Linear/Raycast — command palettes that execute actions ('Change status', 'Assign', 'Send quote', 'Create invoice'), not just navigate. GlobalSearch's result-click path is navigate(path) — it can only jump to records. Henry's 40th-quote-of-the-week workflow is exactly where an action-capable palette compounds: 'Nudge Kristine' / 'Send quote to Greg' / 'Mark complete' — all currently require 3-4 taps through pages.
reproduction_steps: Open src/components/global-search.jsx:128-133: `function go(path) { setOpen(false); setQuery(''); setDebouncedQuery(''); navigate(path); }` — only navigation
Open src/components/global-search.jsx:99-101: allResults is built from records only, not actions
findings.measured_evidence: Code-traced: zero action handlers in global-search.jsx
findings.suggested_fix: Extend GlobalSearch with an action registry: actions = [{label:'New quote', shortcut:'n', run:()=>nav('/app/quotes/new')}, {label:'Nudge...', requiresQuote:true, run:(q)=>openNudgeModal(q)}, ...]. When query matches an action verb, show actions first. Keep existing record search. This is the Phase ι centerpiece.

findings.title: Router Suspense fallback is generic spinner with inline styles — breaks first-paint impression on every lazy route
findings.description: src/app/router.jsx:42-49 defines LoadingFallback as a generic spinner with 'Loading…' text and 6 inline styles. It fires on every route transition for 16 lazy-loaded routes. Kira clicking any sidebar link sees the identical spinner regardless of destination. Inline styles bypass the token system (`fontSize: 14, fontWeight: 600`).
reproduction_steps: Count lazy routes: 16 (PublicQuote, Quote*, Invoice*, Contacts, Bookings, Settings, Billing, Analytics, PaymentsSetup, PaymentsOnboarding, InvoicesList)
findings.competitor_reference: Linear (instant tab switch with optimistic skeleton), Notion (per-page shell)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with per-route skeleton components (route → skeleton map). At minimum move inline styles to a class. Better: pair each lazy() with its own skeleton via a HOC: lazy(() => import('...').then(withSkeleton(DashboardSkeleton))).
findings.resolution_notes: Router's single LoadingFallback replaced with per-route Suspense wrappers. Each of the 19 lazy routes now declares its own destination-specific skeleton component from src/components/skeletons/. Inline styles on LoadingFallback and NotFound moved to CSS classes.

findings.title: Dashboard shows two 'New quote' CTAs above the fold — reduction violation
findings.description: dashboard-page.jsx:611-614 renders <Link className='btn btn-primary'>New quote</Link> in Row 1. Immediately below, lines 619-629 render the dv2-job-form with a 'Build quote / + New quote' button. Both trigger the quote creation flow. Design-system §3 (reduction): 'If two buttons do similar things, one is wrong.' The form input is the richer entry point (accepts a job description and routes directly to the builder's describe phase). The Row-1 button is redundant.
reproduction_steps: Confirm both paths land at /app/quotes/new via trackQuoteFlowStarted and job form submit
findings.competitor_reference: Linear (one create-issue entry point per context)
findings.suggested_fix: Two paths: (a) Remove the Row-1 button; the job form is the entry. (b) Keep the Row-1 button but collapse it into the job-form area on mobile (≤768px). (a) is the reductive choice; (b) is safer if analytics show the Row-1 button carries meaningful click volume.

findings.title: Quote-builder 'tap to change' disclosure uses 10px text — below body legibility standard
findings.description:
src/pages/quote-builder-page.jsx:1056 renders `(tap to change)`. 10px is below the smallest design-system token (--text-2xs is 11px). On iPhone SE with --subtle color, Kira may miss the affordance entirely. The parent summary already implies interactivity via
, so this micro-label may be removable entirely.
findings.suggested_fix: Option A: remove the hint; the <summary> already affords interaction. Option B: use --text-2xs (11px) with --muted color and an inline chevron icon. Option A is cleaner.

findings.title: Quote-builder photo-remove × has zero tap target (padding: 0) on Kira's iPhone SE
findings.description: src/pages/quote-builder-page.jsx:1046 renders `<button ... aria-label='Remove photo' style={{ background: 'none', border: 'none', color: 'var(--muted)', cursor: 'pointer', padding: 0, marginLeft: 4 }}>×</button>`. padding:0 + a × glyph ≈ 14px hitbox. Kira taps to remove the wrong photo, has to re-pick, loses flow.
findings.suggested_fix: Move to a class .jd-helper-dismiss with min-width/height 32px (visible) + ::after expansion to 44x44 target. Replace × with lucide <X> for consistency with UX-002.
findings.resolution_notes: Replaced padding:0 × glyph with .jd-photo-dismiss class: lucide <X size=12>, ::after {inset:-12px} expanding to 44px touch target. Applied in quote-builder-page.jsx and quote-detail-page.jsx.

findings.title: Public-quote error state says 'Contact your contractor' but shows no way to contact them — dead end
findings.description: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:389-398 renders the error state with copy 'This link may have expired. Contact your contractor.' The page provides no contractor phone, email, or name — quote is null when this state fires. Kristine cannot act. The audit's recovery axis: 'no dead ends.' Probable fix requires API support to surface minimal contractor info even for a non-viewable quote (e.g., a public 'quote-lookup' endpoint that returns contractor_name + contact even when the quote itself is expired/not-found).
reproduction_steps: Confirm: `if (error && !quote) return (…)` — so quote is null, no contact info available
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Checkout 'session expired' surfaces a 'Return to merchant' CTA
findings.suggested_fix: Add an API branch to the public quote resolver that returns a minimal `{ contractor_name, contractor_phone?, contractor_email? }` payload even for expired/invalidated tokens (same token, different state). Render a 'Text <name>' button using sms:<phone> URL. This requires additive API work — flag to check against the prompt's hard constraint on public-quote-action.js.
findings.resolution_notes: Created PublicErrorState (src/components/public-error-state.jsx) with Lucide Link2 icon, docType-aware heading, optional sms:/mailto: CTAs. API v1: contractor contact info not yet returned on error responses — v2 task filed.

findings.title: Public-quote conversation uses 👤 and 🔧 as avatars for customer and contractor — Android/iOS render differently
findings.device: Android Pixel vs iOS Safari contrast
findings.description: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:48 — `{entry.role === 'customer' ? '👤' : '🔧'}`. Renders as Google/Apple/MS-styled emoji depending on device. The contractor identity is the most important trust signal on this page; making it a literal wrench emoji is visually weak against Stripe/Apple Pay standards. Worse: the contractor_logo is already available on the quote (used by PublicPageShell) — the conversation could use a contractor initials chip or logo thumbnail.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe / Intercom (named avatars with initials or logos)
findings.suggested_fix: Customer avatar: initials chip from Kristine's name. Contractor avatar: contractor_logo if set, otherwise initials chip of contractor_name. Drop emoji entirely.

findings.title: Public-quote terms <pre> has 9 inline style properties — should be a class
findings.description: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:857 — single <pre> element with inline styles: fontFamily, fontSize 12, lineHeight 1.6, color var, whiteSpace, margin, padding, background var, borderRadius 8, overflowWrap. Nine properties; half use tokens, half are raw numbers. Classic mid-iteration tech debt. No variant re-use across the app.
findings.measured_evidence: 9 inline style props on one element
findings.suggested_fix: Extract to .pq-terms-body class in phase2-public-quote.css. Use --text-xs for size, --space-3 for padding, --r-md for radius.

findings.title: Primary 'Approve & Sign' CTA overrides its own class's fontSize/padding/fontWeight inline
findings.description: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:864 — `<button className='doc-cta-primary pl-cta-approve' style={{ fontSize: 16, padding: '14px 20px', fontWeight: 700 }}>`. Same button pattern at line 647 has NO inline overrides. So one instance of the same CTA looks different from another. 14px is not a --space-* token (tokens are 12/16). Classic case of 'polish without meaning' — the inline was added to fix one rendering but never reconciled back into the class.
reproduction_steps: Open src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:864 (3 overrides on visually identical button)
findings.measured_evidence: Source literal diff between two instances
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the inline overrides. If the sticky CTA needs different sizing than the hero CTA, introduce a .pl-cta-approve--sticky modifier class with the change encoded.

findings.title: E-transfer email rendered at fontSize: 12 on public-quote — copy-critical info for Kristine
findings.description:
src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:899 — `{quote.etransfer_email &&

E-Transfer: {quote.etransfer_email}

}`. Kristine needs to copy this exact email address into her bank app. 12px on iPhone SE with finger-selection is hard to hit precisely. No copy-to-clipboard affordance.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard payout details (copy-to-clipboard on every ID)
findings.suggested_fix: Make the e-transfer email a chip with built-in copy button (Check icon after copy, Lucide Copy icon before). Increase to --text-base. Label clearly as 'Send E-Transfer to:'.
findings.resolution_notes: E-transfer email on public-quote-view replaced with CopyChip component. Renders Copy icon → Check icon transition on click, aria-live "Copied to clipboard" announcement, 1.5s reset. Both etransfer_email render sites updated (payment details block + deposit block). Competitor reference: Stripe payment details copy affordance.

findings.title: Settings toast copy inconsistent across neighboring handlers — voice fracture
findings.description: src/pages/settings-page.jsx shows multiple voice registers: (398) 'Payments connected — you're all set.' (warm, casual); (399) 'Payment setup isn't finished yet. Go to Settings to complete it.' (directive, tells user to go where they already are); (419) 'Could not start Stripe setup. Please try again or contact support.' (corporate apology); (435) 'Could not connect to Stripe. Please try again.' (terse); (452) '8+ characters required' (no object noun); (441) 'Your name is required' (fine). One page, five voices.
reproduction_steps: Open src/pages/settings-page.jsx:398-453 — six distinct toast strings
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard (one voice across the surface)
findings.suggested_fix: Write a 1-page voice guide and sweep. Principles already implied by the good ones: active, specific, never apologize for normal behavior, name the object ('Password needs 8+ characters' not '8+ characters required'). Remove the line 399 redirect — user is already on Settings.

findings.title: Signup step-indicator duplicated inline — 2 identical 8-line hand-rolled dot pairs
findings.description: src/pages/signup-page.jsx:162-165 and 213-216 — two identical step-indicator widgets built from inline-styled divs with 'width: 24, height: 4, borderRadius: 2, background: var(--brand)'. The opacity differs between the two instances to indicate progress. This is a <StepDots current={n} total={2} /> component waiting to happen.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract <StepDots current total />. Move to src/components/ui/step-dots.jsx.

findings.title: Signup 'Skip for now' text-button visually indistinguishable from disclaimer copy
findings.description: src/pages/signup-page.jsx:194-199 — button with background:none, border:none, color:var(--subtle), fontSize:12. Positioned below the primary CTA. Kira's eye reads this as fine-print copy, not an action. If she taps by accident while aiming for the primary, she's navigated past trade setup. Similar pattern appears in quote-builder-page.jsx:1066 ('or add items manually →').
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe onboarding (skip links are always labeled as links with underline + chevron, never look like disclaimers)
findings.suggested_fix: Render as a standard .btn-link: underline on hover, --text-sm, --muted color (not --subtle), with a right chevron. Apply the same pattern to quote-builder-page.jsx:1066.

findings.title: index.css is 7,492 lines — exceeds the design system's own acknowledged debt target of ~6,300
findings.description: docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md §9 flags '6,300-line index.css needs modular split' as a Phase 6 deferral. Actual current size: 7,492 lines. The debt grew between design-system write and v100 ship. 2,066 raw px values inside it (not tokens). Bundle-size impact is real (this file is CSS-loaded, not lazy).
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard (split by feature, lazy-loaded, each < 50KB gzipped)
findings.suggested_fix: Not an elevation task — this is a refactor. Recommend: extract the top 5 feature-specific sections (dashboard v1, quote-detail, invoice-detail, public-quote, quote-builder) into sibling CSS files lazy-loaded via their page's dynamic import. Phase 6 work, not α-ι.

findings.title: Skeleton adoption low: 7 of 25 pages use a skeleton; 11 use a spinner as sole loading state
findings.description: grep-measured: 7 pages import Skeleton or PageSkeleton (bookings, quotes-list, invoices-list, invoice-detail via local impl, contacts, analytics, dashboard via local CardSkeleton). The remaining 18 either have no loading state (rely on empty render), or use loading-spinner+Loading… text (project-portal-page 4x, public-quote-page, billing-page, public-invoice-page 2x, pricing-page, quote-builder-page, public-amendment-page, additional-work-detail-page). The audit standard: 'every data-dependent page' gets a skeleton matching real layout.
findings.suggested_fix: Build a library of per-page skeleton components in src/components/skeletons/. Match each page's primary layout. Audit spinner call-sites one-by-one.
findings.resolution_notes: Skeleton library built in src/components/skeletons/ (skel-base.jsx + index.jsx with 14 named skeletons). invoice-detail-page, additional-work-detail-page, and quote-detail-page inline spinners/minimal-skeletons replaced with proper zone-matched skeleton components. Pages already using PageSkeleton (contacts, analytics, bookings, invoices-list, billing) were compliant and not changed. dashboard-page CardSkeleton left in place (deferred to Phase 4 primitive extraction). quote-builder AI-build spinner is intentional UX, not a loading state.

findings.title: project-portal-page.jsx has inline '#ea580c' and '#fef3c7' and '#f4f4f5' hex colors on a customer-facing page
findings.theme: all — but especially dark theme will look wrong
findings.description: src/pages/project-portal-page.jsx:76, 335, 341, 426 — hardcoded hex colors on a customer-facing surface that should theme. '#ea580c' is Tailwind orange-600. '#fef3c7' is Tailwind amber-100, '#92400e' is amber-800, '#f4f4f5' is zinc-100, '#71717a' is zinc-500. These are Tailwind defaults — someone patched in colors from a different palette instead of using --doc-* tokens that exist for exactly this surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with --doc-accent, --doc-warn, --doc-muted-bg etc. Audit the full --doc-* palette for coverage gaps and add tokens where missing.

findings.title: quote-builder-page.jsx:1224 is a single JSX line with multiple buttons and window-global access
findings.description: src/pages/quote-builder-page.jsx:1224 is a ~700-character single line rendering three add-item triggers ('Search catalog', '+ Custom item', 'Ask Foreman') with a conditional fourth ('Retry AI scope'). Contains a window.__punchlistOpenForeman global access, inline context construction, and a complex ternary. This is a maintainability issue but also a craft issue — the formatting is evidence of 'iteration without consolidation.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extract <AddItemTriggers quoteContext={...} onMode={setAddMode} onRetry={retryScope} /> component. Eliminate window.__punchlistOpenForeman in favor of a useForeman() hook or context provider.

findings.title: Quote-builder textarea has no auto-grow and no character counter — Kira's long description scrolls internally
findings.description: src/pages/quote-builder-page.jsx:1034 — textarea with rows={4}. No auto-height, no character count, no helper showing description length. Kira at 9pm types a 6-line description and the box scrolls internally, hiding what she wrote 3 sentences ago. Anxiety amplifier.
reproduction_steps: Confirm no onInput auto-resize, no maxLength, no counter
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard / Linear / Notion — auto-grow textareas throughout
findings.suggested_fix: Add useAutoGrow() hook driven by scrollHeight. Cap at viewport/2 on mobile to preserve fold-line. Optionally show '142 chars — nice and specific' helper for positive reinforcement on ≥80 chars.
findings.resolution_notes: useAutoGrow hook (scrollHeight-driven, capped at viewport/2) wired to describe textarea via descTextareaRef. qb-desc-auto CSS class adds overflow:hidden + transition. findings.title: terms-page.jsx headings use fontSize: 14 inline on seven sibling h3s — headings styled as body text

findings.title: Dashboard Row-1 New Quote button uses .btn-primary without .btn-lg — visual weight below the job-input 'Build quote' button beneath it

findings.title: ActionListRow button scale :active animation lacks prefers-reduced-motion override (global only zeroes durations, not the transform state)
findings.description: src/styles/dashboard-v2.css:481 — `.dv2-arow-btn:active { transform: scale(0.97); }`. The tokens.css reduced-motion override (line 225-240) zeroes transition-duration. With duration 0ms the scale state is still applied but visually instant. That is acceptable — scale(0.97) held briefly isn't vestibular. However, the audit's 'every new animation respects prefers-reduced-motion' rule deserves a defensive check here because the transform is load-bearing feedback, not decorative. Marking this as P3 because the global override likely mitigates the effect.
findings.measured_evidence: Code-traced; global override exists and covers the common case
findings.suggested_fix: Optional — wrap the :active transform in an @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) block. Low priority.
findings.resolution_notes: Added explicit @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.dv2-arow-btn:active{transform:none}} override. Defensive hardening beyond the global token duration-zeroing — eliminates any held scale state for vestibular-sensitive users.

findings.title: ActionSheet placeholder copy has filler — 'the more detail the better…' violates voice guide
findings.description: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:79 — `'Describe what you\'d like changed — the more detail the better…'`. Filler phrase. findings.title: Dashboard `New quote` Row-1 button label lacks first-person warmth present elsewhere
findings.description: dashboard-page.jsx:613 'New quote'. The signup CTA 'Create my first quote →' (signup-page.jsx:192) demonstrates the warmer voice this product is capable of. For returning contractors the label doesn't need first-person — but the job-form button at line 624-628 swaps between 'Build quote' and '+ New quote' depending on input state. Consistency between the two 'New quote' triggers (when they co-exist) would be a win.
findings.competitor_reference: Superhuman send confirmations ('Kristine's phone just buzzed')
findings.suggested_fix: Likely becomes moot after UX-008. If the Row-1 button stays, keep 'New quote' terse; the personality lives elsewhere. Worth raising so the designer makes this call consciously, not accidentally.

findings.title: BUG: initial_sms fallback template contains literal '{fn?\' \'+fn:\'\'}' that .replace('{firstName}') won't match
findings.page_or_flow: flow #1 step 'send quote' (fallback path when DB template fetch fails)
findings.frequency: rare (only fires when userTemplates is empty AND DB lookup failed — but high-impact when it fires; Kristine receives a broken text)
findings.description: src/pages/quote-detail-page.jsx:151 contains: `const tmplBody = userTemplates?.find(t=>t.template_key==='initial_sms')?.body || \`Hi{fn?' '+fn:''}, your quote from {senderName} is ready: {link}\`;` — the fallback template literal on the right side of || contains the string `{fn?' '+fn:''}` which is NOT a valid JS interpolation (it's inside a backtick but uses `{...}` not `${...}`). Then `.replace('{firstName}', fn)` only replaces the token `{firstName}` which the fallback does not contain. If the fallback path fires, Kristine receives literally: 'Hi{fn?\' \'+fn:\'\'}, your quote from Acme Plumbing is ready: https://...'. Discovered while auditing the single-line handler formatting (UX-037); would likely not have been caught in review because the line is ~2000 characters wide.
reproduction_steps: Trace: if userTemplates is null/empty, tmplBody = fallback string
Simulate: tmplBody = "Hi{fn?' '+fn:''}, ..."; msg = tmplBody.replace('{firstName}', 'Kristine') → result still contains the broken token
findings.measured_evidence: Source inspection of fallback string and subsequent .replace chain
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the fallback template with one using the same {firstName} / {senderName} / {link} token vocabulary as the primary template: `Hi{firstName ? ' ' + firstName : ''}, your quote from {senderName} is ready: {link}`. But wait — even that won't work with .replace because the ternary is in the template. The correct fix: use explicit token `{firstName}` and let .replace substitute, or switch to renderTemplate() from src/lib/api/templates.js which appears to be the single-source template renderer. The cleanest fix is the latter — drop the fallback literal and rely on renderTemplate() with a known-good system default.
findings.resolution_notes: Replaced literal JS ternary fallback string with getSystemDefaults().initial_sms routed through renderTemplate(). Single token-resolution path. handleCopyLink and handleDownloadPdf reformatted to multiline.

findings.title: 4 public pages have copy-pasted loading-spinner and 🔗 error blocks — extract shared components
findings.description: src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:383-398, public-invoice-page.jsx:72-100, public-amendment-page.jsx:88-104, project-portal-page.jsx:1217-1232 — all four render nearly-identical loading+error scaffolds: doc-shell wrapper, centered flex, spinner or 🔗 glyph, 'unavailable' headline, 'Contact your contractor' fallback. Each slightly differs: amendment tacks on raw error text at fontSize:11; additional-work uses a different shell (UX-033). This is 4x copy-paste that compounds UX-003 (spinner→skeleton) and UX-011 (error dead-end) — fixing at component level fixes all 4 pages simultaneously.
reproduction_steps: Each is ~12-16 lines of near-identical JSX with variant copy
findings.suggested_fix: Extract src/components/public-loading-state.jsx with props {contractorName, logoUrl, label}. Extract src/components/public-error-state.jsx with props {contractorName, contractorPhone, contractorEmail, docType, rawError}. Replace at call sites. This delivers UX-003 + UX-011 fixes across all 4 pages in one pass.
findings.resolution_notes: Extracted PublicLoadingState and PublicErrorState. Replaced copy-pasted blocks in all 5 public pages. public-additional-work-page migrated from marketing-shell to doc-shell via shared components (UX-033 partial, full treatment deferred to Phase 4).

findings.title: public-additional-work-page uses 'marketing-shell' while sibling public pages use 'doc-shell' — visual shell inconsistency
findings.description: src/pages/public-additional-work-page.jsx:48 renders `<div className='marketing-shell'><main className='container'>…` for the loading state (and presumably elsewhere). The 3 other public pages (public-quote, public-invoice, public-amendment) use `<div className='doc-shell'><div className='doc-container'>…`. Kristine who has received quote + amendment + additional-work links from the same contractor will see one visually different scaffold. findings.title: project-portal primary nav tabs are 4 emoji (📋 📝 💳 💬) — Kristine's main way to move around post-approval
findings.description: src/pages/project-portal-page.jsx:1244-1249 — tabs = [{id:'quote',icon:'📋'},{id:'updates',icon:'📝'},{id:'payments',icon:'💳'},{id:'messages',icon:'💬'}]. reproduction_steps: Confirm TabBar at line 19 passes t.icon directly into <span className='pp-tab-icon'>

findings.title: project-portal MessagesTab compose input and send button have 7 + 5 inline style properties — propagates the 'polish without meaning' pattern
findings.description: src/pages/project-portal-page.jsx:1141 — text input with inline `flex: 1, padding: '10px 14px', borderRadius: 10, border: '1px solid var(--doc-line)', fontSize: 14, outline: 'none', background: 'var(--doc-card-bg, #fff)'`. Line 1143 — send button with `padding: '10px 18px', fontSize: 14, border: 'none', cursor: 'pointer', flexShrink: 0` overriding its doc-cta-primary class. findings.title: quote-detail customer contact buttons are pure emoji (📞 💬 ✉) — Henry's most-frequent action surface
findings.description: src/pages/quote-detail-page.jsx:280-282 — three anchor buttons with no visible text, only emoji icons: `📞` and similar for 💬 (SMS) and ✉ (email). Henry opens this page 20-40 times per day (his v99 baseline from the changelog); these are his primary means of reaching a customer. Emoji-as-anchor-button is worst-case for brand consistency — each one renders as OS-default glyph with no control over weight/color/theme. Additionally ✉ (U+2709) is a monochrome dingbat while 📞 and 💬 are color emoji — inconsistent weights within a single button row.
findings.competitor_reference: Linear contact-card icons, Pipedrive activity row icons (all SVG with color control)
findings.measured_evidence: 3 emoji anchors in a critical location
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with lucide-react: Phone, MessageSquare, Mail. Keep aria-label. Preserve btn-secondary btn-sm classes. This is a 3-line change with outsized perception impact.

findings.title: quote-detail-page.jsx handlers at lines 151, 154, 156, 160 are single-line with ~20 statements each — bugs hide in code this dense (UX-031 is an instance)
findings.description: Lines 151 (~2000 chars, handleSendText), 154 (~680 chars, handleCopyLink), 156 (~1200 chars, handleDownloadPdf), 160 (~900 chars, completeAndInvoice) are each single-line functions with embedded comments, nested try/catch, inline state updates, and conditional chains. This is not a 'nice to have' readability concern — UX-031 is a live bug that escaped review, and the density of the code is a contributing factor. Related: the same file has multiple ~1600-char single-line JSX expressions in phase banners (lines 288, 290).
findings.suggested_fix: Format with Prettier (max-len ~100). Likely reveals 2-3 more small bugs. Tie to UX-031 fix — fixing just UX-031 without reformatting around it leaves the same fragility for the next edit.

findings.title: public-invoice e-transfer copy button has no post-click feedback — Kristine taps, nothing visible happens
findings.description: src/pages/public-invoice-page.jsx:390-394 — `<button onClick={() => { navigator.clipboard?.writeText(invoice.etransfer_email); }} style={{ fontSize: 11, padding: '2px 8px', marginLeft: 4, cursor: 'pointer' }}>Copy</button>`. No feedback: no toast, no icon swap (Check), no aria-live announcement. Kristine taps, unsure whether it worked. Has to switch to her bank app and paste to find out. Additionally: 11px with 2/8px padding is a ~16px tall button — below touch-target standard. Labels the button 'Copy' (with verb, good) but provides nothing after.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard 'Copy' patterns (swap icon Copy→Check for 1.5s, announce via aria-live)
findings.measured_evidence: Source — no .then() handler, no state update
findings.suggested_fix: Add useState for copied state. onClick awaits clipboard write then sets copied=true, setTimeout to reset at 1.5s. Swap Copy icon → Check icon, add aria-live='polite' region with 'Copied to clipboard'. Increase visible size — 32px min height on mobile.
findings.resolution_notes: public-invoice-page e-transfer copy button replaced with CopyChip. Previously: bare button with navigator.clipboard, no visual feedback. Now: Copy→Check icon swap, green copied state, aria-live announcement, 1.5s auto-reset. Competitor reference: Linear copy-to-clipboard interactions.

findings.title: public-invoice payment CTAs fragment across 4 inline-styled variants (Connect, Stripe link, Square, PayPal) — with hardcoded PayPal blue #0070ba
findings.description: src/pages/public-invoice-page.jsx:335, 354, 359, 364 — four primary-CTA buttons for different payment providers, each with inline style overrides. Line 364 has hardcoded `background: '#0070ba'` (PayPal blue) and `color: '#fff'`. Line 359 has `background: 'var(--doc-text)', color: '#fff'` (Square, borrowing the body-text color token as a background — semantically wrong). Different sizes (line 335: fontSize 15, padding 14/18; lines 354-364: fontSize 14, padding 12/16). Kristine with 2+ payment options sees CTAs that look inconsistent in weight and color.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Checkout, Shopify Pay (unified CTA design, brand lockups as icons only)
findings.measured_evidence: 4 CTAs × 5-7 inline style props each; 1 hardcoded hex
findings.suggested_fix: Unify CTAs as a single .doc-cta-primary with a provider icon prefix (lucide Stripe-branded SVG or a local /public/icons/paypal.svg etc.). Sizes consistent. Provider brand color lives on a small icon, not as the button background. This also fixes theming — PayPal blue as a background in dark mode is broken.

findings.title: public-invoice 'Powered by Punchlist · Secure checkout via Stripe' rendered at fontSize: 10 — trust copy at illegible size
findings.description: src/pages/public-invoice-page.jsx:348 — `Powered by Punchlist · Secure checkout via Stripe`. 10px, muted color. This is TRUST copy (Stripe brand lockup) appearing right under a payment CTA — the exact moment Kristine asks herself 'is this a real checkout?'. Stripe's own brand guidelines mandate minimum readable size for their attribution. Also: 10px is below --text-2xs (11px), the design system's smallest sanctioned size.
findings.suggested_fix: Raise to --text-xs (12px) minimum; ideally --text-sm (13px). Consider: a Stripe brand-approved shield icon + 'Secured by Stripe' at --text-sm. Also applies next to financing tile copy at line 343 (fontSize: 11).

findings.title: public-amendment error state surfaces raw technical error string below 'contact your contractor' — voice + legibility double-fail
findings.description:
src/pages/public-amendment-page.jsx:101 — `

{error}

` where `error` is the raw Error.message (e.g. 'Server error', 'Amendment not found', 'Failed'). Kristine sees two messages: a friendly 'This link may be invalid or expired' and below it a technical debug string at illegible size. Two voices. Also doesn't help her recover.

findings.resolution_notes: Raw error string removed. PublicErrorState (UX-032) never renders error variable content — only user-appropriate copy.

findings.title: project-portal TermsSection <pre> has the identical 6-property inline-style cluster as public-quote-page:857 — exact copy-paste
findings.page_or_flow: project-portal quote tab terms + public-quote terms
findings.description: src/pages/project-portal-page.jsx:48 `<pre style={{ fontFamily: 'inherit', fontSize: 13, lineHeight: 1.7, color: 'var(--doc-text)', whiteSpace: 'pre-wrap', margin: '12px 0' }}>` is a near-duplicate of src/pages/public-quote-page.jsx:857 (which was flagged as UX-013). Note minor diff: 13 vs 12 fontSize, 1.7 vs 1.6 lineHeight. Even within a near-duplicate the numbers drift — classic sign that both files are edited independently. Bundles with UX-013 into a single .terms-body class fix.

findings.title: STRENGTH — The 8 system default SMS templates are top-1% content design; treat as canonical voice reference
findings.description: src/lib/api/templates.js:31-48 defines SYSTEM_DEFAULTS — 8 templates that exemplify the voice Punchlist should hold throughout the app. Specific craft moments: followup_2 uses face-saving phrasing ('didn't get buried'), followup_3 uses takeaway close ('totally understand if the timing isn't right'), deposit_received_sms combines gratitude + concrete next step + 'I'll take it from here' ownership language, job_complete asks a soft question rather than declaring completion. Further: TEMPLATE_HINTS (lines 76-85) explain the psychology of each template in Settings so contractors understand *why* changing them might weaken reply rate. This is meta-delight — the app teaches the craft it embodies. Log as strength + use as canonical voice exemplar for Phase θ.
reproduction_steps: Compare to settings-page.jsx:1131 warning banner copy ('⚠ Add your phone number above') — flat by comparison
Compare to settings-page.jsx:1147 '$0.01/text' disclosure — flatter still, at 10px
findings.competitor_reference: Superhuman onboarding flow, Linear ship-log entries
findings.suggested_fix: Treat this file as the voice canon. When Phase θ writes docs/VOICE-GUIDE.md, quote these 8 templates and their 8 hints as the exemplars. Every other surface in the app should be held to this standard.

findings.title: project-portal QuoteTab duplicates ~500 lines of state/handlers from public-quote-page.jsx — parallel maintenance risk
findings.description: src/pages/project-portal-page.jsx QuoteTab (line 122-690) re-implements handleConnectPay (line 144), deposit polling (line 157-176), toggleOptional (line 194-200), openSignature, handleSheetSubmit — all near-identical to public-quote-page.jsx equivalents. This means: (a) every public-quote fix (including the Phase α PublicErrorState work from UX-032) has to be applied twice; (b) divergences will accumulate because each page iterates separately; (c) UX-013's terms inline-style cluster appears in both places (UX-042); (d) emoji avatars on conversation UI appear in both. The 'one URL per customer-job' project-portal pattern was introduced to REPLACE legacy public pages, but QuoteTab is a fork, not an abstraction. Compounds UX-032, UX-042, and whatever future public-quote-page fixes land.

Shared state management via the same component. This also resolves UX-042 (terms <pre>) in one place and sets up UX-032 shared components to benefit both pages. Large refactor — recommended for Phase δ, but worth flagging now because every in-between fix doubles the work.

findings.title: UpdatesTab's AmendmentCard and AdditionalWorkCard duplicate logic from standalone public-amendment-page.jsx and public-additional-work-page.jsx
findings.description: src/pages/project-portal-page.jsx AmendmentCard (line 753-831) and AdditionalWorkCard (line 832-905) re-implement the amendment-approve/decline flow and additional-work-approve/decline flow that live in the standalone public pages (public-amendment-page.jsx, public-additional-work-page.jsx). Three places for the same logic: standalone amendment page, project-portal AmendmentCard, and the quote-detail amendment-diff component. Same pattern as UX-044 but across amendments and AW.
reproduction_steps: Compare handleAmendmentAction in project-portal-page.jsx:716 to the equivalent in public-amendment-page.jsx
Both POST to /api/public-amendment with same action/token shape
findings.measured_evidence: Three implementation sites for two flows
findings.suggested_fix: Same pattern as UX-044 — extract <AmendmentView mode> and <AdditionalWorkView mode> components with shared state/handlers. After extraction, standalone pages become thin wrappers, portal tabs render the same component.

findings.title: Settings has two save-state indicators — top-right pill AND bottom-row label — redundant
findings.description: src/pages/settings-page.jsx:508-514 renders a save-state pill in the AppShell actions slot: '✓ Saved' / '⟳ Saving…' / '● Unsaved'. Line 1285-1297 renders an additional save row at the bottom of the page: spinner + 'Saving…' or '✓ Changes save automatically' + a 'Save now' button. Two widgets communicating overlapping state. Kira learning where Save happens has to reconcile the two. Either the top pill is the canonical save indicator (drop the bottom row, or simplify to just 'Save now' button) or the bottom is (drop the top pill).
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard (single save-state indicator, usually inline per-section)
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the top-right pill — it's visible from any scroll position and already elegant. Drop the bottom row's save-state label, keeping only the 'Save now' button for users who prefer explicit control.

findings.title: Settings Notifications tab has inline Tailwind rgba alert banners — amber (#92400e) and green (#15803d) hardcoded, not themed
findings.theme: all (alerts won't theme for dark mode)
findings.description: src/pages/settings-page.jsx:1131-1133 warning banner with `background: 'rgba(245,158,11,.06)', border: '1px solid rgba(245,158,11,.15)', color: '#92400e'`. Line 1135-1138 success banner symmetric with `rgba(19,138,91,.04)`, `rgba(19,138,91,.12)`, `#15803d`. These are Tailwind amber-500 and green-700 colors as raw rgba/hex. Same pattern as UX-021 on project-portal but this time on the contractor's own settings page. Breaks theming and propagates the ad-hoc-palette problem.

Replace at call sites. Also ideal candidates for a reusable <Alert variant='warn'|'success'> primitive in src/components/ui/.

findings.title: 'Standard messaging rates apply. Approximately $0.01/text' at fontSize: 10 in --subtle — material pricing disclosure at illegible size
findings.description:
src/pages/settings-page.jsx:1145-1147: `
Standard messaging rates apply. Approximately $0.01/text.
`. This is material pricing information — Kira is being told her business will be charged per SMS. Rendering it at 10px in --subtle is the industry-wide UX antipattern where 'disclose but hide' is used to minimize friction at the cost of informed consent. It's also a trust breaker when Kira realizes mid-use that she's being charged.
findings.competitor_reference: Twilio, Stripe Billing (pricing information at readable size with link to 'learn more')
findings.suggested_fix: Raise to --text-xs (12px) minimum, ideally --text-sm. Keep --muted color (not --subtle). Consider phrasing: 'SMS is priced separately at approximately $0.01 per text. See pricing for details.' with a link. This is also a voice finding for Phase θ — the current phrasing uses 'Standard messaging rates apply' which is telco-contract legalese, not Punchlist voice.
findings.resolution_notes: Removed fontSize:10 + color:var(--subtle) inline overrides from SMS cost disclosure. Now uses muted small class at --text-xs / --muted. Copy rewritten: "SMS is priced separately at approximately $0.01 per text."

findings.title: Settings Account delete-account button renders danger color 3 different ways — btn needs .btn--danger modifier family
findings.description: src/pages/settings-page.jsx:1247 'Delete my account' — `className='btn btn-secondary'` with `style={{ color: 'var(--danger)' }}`. Line 1253 'Yes, I understand' — `className='btn btn-secondary'` with `style={{ color: 'var(--danger)', borderColor: 'var(--danger)' }}`. Line 1261 'Permanently delete everything' — `className='btn btn-primary'` with `style={{ background: 'var(--danger)', borderColor: 'var(--danger)' }}`. Three buttons escalating destructiveness, each implemented with a different combination of classes and inline style overrides. Functional, but a .btn--danger-ghost / .btn--danger-outline / .btn--danger-solid modifier family would encode the intent once and make the escalation visually systematic.
findings.suggested_fix: Add .btn--danger-ghost (text only), .btn--danger-outline (border + text), .btn--danger-solid (filled) to the button system. Also flag: this escalation pattern is genuinely good — preserve the 3-step confirm, just clean up the styling.

findings.title: Preferences tab has a hand-rolled toggle switch (10 lines of inline style) — should be a <Toggle> primitive
findings.description: src/pages/settings-page.jsx:1174-1211 hand-rolls an iOS-style toggle with role='switch' aria-checked: absolutely-positioned knob, 10 inline style properties, manual left transition, #fff hardcoded on the knob. The a11y is correct (role + aria-checked) but this is a component-extraction opportunity. The codebase already has design-system primitives (Card, Stat, etc.) in src/components/ui/ — add <Toggle checked onChange disabled ariaLabel />. Likely this exact switch exists or will exist elsewhere (template editor, future preferences, etc.) — extracting now prevents divergence.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract src/components/ui/toggle.jsx with the exact visual spec. Drop-in replace. Also add --min-tap-44 utility to ensure the tap target is ≥44px (the current 44×24 visible control is below that on height). Hitbox expansion via ::before pseudo-element.

findings.title: STRENGTH — Preferences reflective-summary 'Currently: X is on/off' pattern confirms Kira's choice back to her
findings.description: src/pages/settings-page.jsx:1213-1217: after the auto-send-invoice toggle, a panel reads `💡 Currently: Invoice is sent automatically when you complete a job.` (or the negation when off). This reflects Kira's selection back to her in plain language, removing any ambiguity about which way the switch goes. Often toggles confuse users because they show the setting name, not the current consequence. This pattern inverts that. Propagate to other toggle-heavy surfaces (Notifications tab, future preference panels). Also aligns with the voice canon established by UX-043.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard policy-settings (similar reflective summary pattern)
findings.suggested_fix: Preserve. Propagate: audit other toggle surfaces (SMS notifications enable/disable, digest enable/disable, push enable/disable) and add 'Currently: X' reflective summaries where missing. Minus the 💡 emoji — per UX-002.
findings.resolution_notes: Reflective "Currently: X" pattern propagated to: (1) Daily Digest panel — states on/off clearly, (2) SMS Notifications panel — states on/off with phone number context, (3) Auto-send invoice panel — existing 💡 emoji replaced with <Info size={14}> + pl-reflective-summary shared CSS class. All three use consistent pl-reflective-summary component pattern.

findings.title: Settings About panel uses double-hyphen '--' instead of em-dash '—' — punctuation inconsistency with app-wide convention
findings.description: src/pages/settings-page.jsx:1276: 'Built for speed, clarity, and customer trust -- not to replace your accounting software.' Double-hyphen '--' where the rest of the app uses proper em-dashes '—'. Micro-voice finding but emblematic of the broader voice-inconsistency issue: the voice is good when attended to (UX-043) but drifts in places that haven't been reviewed recently. A typography-aware copy pass for Phase θ should catch all of these.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '--' with '—'. Sweep the rest of the codebase for ' -- ' (with spaces) to catch siblings.

findings.title: project-portal PaymentsTab deposit badge has inline Tailwind hex (#fef3c7, #92400e) — same leakage pattern as UX-021 and UX-047
findings.theme: all (badge won't theme in dark mode)
findings.description: src/pages/project-portal-page.jsx:953: `<span className='pp-update-type' style={{ background: depositPaid ? 'var(--doc-green-soft)' : '#fef3c7', color: depositPaid ? 'var(--doc-green)' : '#92400e' }}>`. When the deposit is paid, uses --doc-* tokens; when pending, falls back to raw Tailwind amber. Asymmetric. The pending state will break in dark mode. Same Tailwind-palette leakage as UX-021 (project-portal checkbox) and UX-047 (settings alerts) — consistent enough to suggest this app once used Tailwind amber/green directly before the --doc-* tokens were added, and the migration was incomplete.
findings.measured_evidence: Source — asymmetric token/hex usage in one ternary
findings.suggested_fix: Add --doc-amber-soft, --doc-amber, etc. to the --doc-* token family. Replace the inline hex. Audit for other Tailwind-palette leakage with: grep -rn '#fef3c7\|#92400e\|#15803d\|#0070ba' src/ — gather these together as a Phase δ sub-sweep.

findings.title: STRENGTH — followup-modal reassurance footer 'You're the sender — this goes out as your message, not from Punchlist'
findings.description:
src/components/followup-modal.jsx:251-253: `

You're the sender — this goes out as your message, not from Punchlist.

`. This one line addresses the exact anxiety a contractor has about automated nudges: 'will this sound robotic?' Punchlist clarifies ownership — it's Kira's message, in her voice, using her phone identity. This is the reassurance pattern that makes the feature feel like a tool, not a bot. Log as strength + propagate: the SMS template preview on quote-detail M5 §5.1 ('Texted to Kristine' + preview card) should include similar reassurance language around auto-sent deposit receipts etc.
findings.suggested_fix: Preserve. Propagate pattern to other auto-send surfaces (deposit receipt confirmation in Settings, invoice auto-send toggle preview, any future auto-messages).
findings.resolution_notes: Sender-reassurance "goes out as your message, not from Punchlist" propagated to: (1) auto-send invoice settings panel (shown when toggle is on), (2) quote send modal text path (pl-sender-reassurance shared class). Original in followup-modal.jsx migrated to pl-sender-reassurance class. Competitor reference: Superhuman sender-identity clarity.

findings.title: STRENGTH — followup-modal SMS character counter is segment-boundary-aware (160/320 thresholds with '2 SMS segments' warning)
findings.description: src/components/followup-modal.jsx:208-223: character counter not only shows current count but WARNS at 160 chars (SMS 2-segment boundary — each segment is a separate telco charge) and CAPS at 320. Color shifts: muted under 160, amber at 160+, red at 280+. Meta-helper text: 'Quote link is added automatically if missing' so Kira doesn't paste the link twice. This is the SMS-aware-UX equivalent of Stripe's credit-card-type-detection: solves a problem Kira didn't know existed. Top-1% craft.
findings.competitor_reference: Twilio Studio (segment-aware SMS composer), Front SMS app
findings.suggested_fix: Preserve. Lift the segment-boundary-awareness + char counter into a reusable <SmsComposerField> primitive for use on the Messages template editor and any future SMS-composition surface.
findings.resolution_notes: SmsComposerField primitive extracted to src/components/ui/SmsComposerField.jsx. Props: id, value, onChange, rows, label, showLinkHint, autoFocus, disabled, className. Segment-boundary awareness: warn color at 160+, "2 SMS segments" label at 160+, "At limit" at 320. Migrated into: followup-modal.jsx (primary call site), quote-builder send modal SMS composer. Barrel-exported from ui/index.js.

findings.title: Analytics StatCard reimplements the <Stat> primitive that the design system already provides
findings.description: src/pages/analytics-page.jsx:29-43 defines a local StatCard with inline styles: 18px 20px padding, fontSize 11 label, fontSize 26 value, letterSpacing -.03em, fontWeight 800. docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:162 documents a <Stat> primitive with label/value/prefix/suffix/tone props, count-up enabled by default, width-reserved via --min-ch. Stat is imported and used on signup (src/pages/quote-builder-page.jsx:28) and elsewhere. Analytics — the numeric-typography showcase — reimplements what already exists. Also skips the <Stat>'s tabular-nums + count-up animations. This is the canonical 'adoption debt' finding from UX-001 but on a high-visibility page.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard (canonical Stat rendering across all analytics)
findings.measured_evidence: Two implementations of the same widget; only one exists in the design system
findings.suggested_fix: Replace local StatCard with <Stat label={...} value={...} prefix={...} suffix={...} tone={...} hint={sub} />. Remove the local function. Gains: count-up animation on load, tabular-nums on numbers, width reservation (stops layout shift as numbers update). Delete analytics-page's local StatCard entirely.

findings.title: Analytics chart palette is 5 hardcoded Tailwind hex colors — no theme support, no token
findings.theme: dark theme will render them as-is (not remapped)
findings.description: src/pages/analytics-page.jsx:48: `const colors = ['#e86b30', '#f59e0b', '#3b82f6', '#059669', '#8b5cf6'];`. Punchlist brand orange + Tailwind amber-500, blue-500, emerald-600, violet-500. Hardcoded. Won't respect brand theming, won't respect dark-mode adjustments (in light mode these saturated colors look punchy; in dark mode they look garish against a dark bg without saturation adjustment). Charts are inherently color-carrying — they need a defined palette, not ad-hoc hex.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to src/styles/tokens.css: --chart-1 through --chart-5 with light and dark theme variants. Replace the hardcoded array with `const colors = ['var(--chart-1)', ...]` or consume via CSS custom properties. Also: sort by information-hierarchy meaning — pipeline funnel should have a consistent directional palette (e.g., orange→green as funnel progresses).

findings.title: Analytics FunnelChart renders each row with ~20 inline style properties — should be a class-based component
findings.description: src/pages/analytics-page.jsx:45-74 — the entire FunnelChart is inline-styled. Each bar row has 7+ inline style properties. Animation inline (transition: 'width .5s ease'). Chart primitive can't be reused outside analytics-page. If bookings or invoices want a similar visualization, this gets copy-pasted. Same anti-pattern as UX-050 (hand-rolled toggle) — a component that should be in src/components/ui/.
findings.competitor_reference: Recharts, Chart.js (or a lightweight local component factored correctly)
findings.suggested_fix: Extract src/components/ui/funnel-chart.jsx with props {data, palette, showPercentages}. Classes in dedicated CSS. Consumable by analytics, future reports, etc. Takes UX-057 palette with it.

findings.title: STRENGTH — Bookings page is the most design-system-compliant page audited (3 fontSize violations in 622 lines)
findings.description: Grep comparison across audited pages: bookings-page.jsx has 3 inline fontSize violations in 622 lines. Compare to settings (33), pricing (40), payments-onboarding (43), project-portal (64), analytics (21). Bookings uses classes almost exclusively. Also: PageSkeleton on load, EmptyState-aligned voice ('All caught up — no jobs waiting'), proper role='button' + tabIndex + onKeyDown for clickable rails, aria-label for rail toggle. This is what post-Phase-δ-target pages look like. Use bookings as the reference page for Phase δ's token-discipline sweep.
findings.suggested_fix: Preserve. Use as the reference page for Phase δ — every other page's token-discipline sweep should aim for similar violation density (≤ 0.5 per 100 lines).

findings.title: StepDots widget exists in 3 places (signup-page inline ×2, payments-onboarding-page as local function) — extract to design-system primitive
findings.description: Compounds UX-017 (signup). signup-page.jsx:162-165 and 213-216 are inline step-indicators (two copies in one file). payments-onboarding-page.jsx:54-72 is a proper StepDots function but file-local — not exported. Three places, three slightly different implementations. payments-onboarding version has the richest spec (current dot is 24×8 rounded rect; done/pending are 8×8 dots; opacity shift for done). Use THAT as the canonical implementation, extract to src/components/ui/step-dots.jsx, drop into signup and anywhere else.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract src/components/ui/step-dots.jsx from the payments-onboarding version (best spec). Replace 3 call sites. Export from the ui barrel. This is a bundled fix with UX-017.

findings.title: Invoice-detail edit-mode inputs have repeated inline font/padding overrides on every row — tap targets too small
findings.description: src/pages/invoice-detail-page.jsx:345-348 — each row in the edit table has `.input` class PLUS inline `fontSize: 12, padding: '6px 8px'`. Line 348 remove button has `padding: '4px 6px', fontSize: 11`. On iPhone SE, an 11px button with 4px/6px padding gives ~16px visible target. Below 44px. Same pattern as UX-005 and UX-010. Also: why override the .input class? Because the designer wanted smaller inputs in this table — but the override pattern means Phase δ can't globally improve .input without watching for every inline override.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Dashboard invoice editor (consistent control sizing)
findings.measured_evidence: 4 inputs × 2 inline style props; 1 button with 2 inline style props; per row, ×N rows
findings.suggested_fix: Add .input--dense modifier (fontSize: var(--text-sm), padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3)). Add .btn--xs with hitbox-expanding pseudo-element for remove-action. Replace inline overrides.
findings.resolution_notes: Added .input--dense modifier (var(--text-xs), var(--space-2) var(--space-3)) and .btn--xs with ::after {inset:-10px -8px} tap target. Replaced 3x inline fontSize/padding overrides per invoice-edit-row. Remove button now uses lucide X + btn--xs.

findings.title: Pricing-page has 40 inline fontSize violations in 227 lines — second-worst density in the app
findings.description: grep: 40 inline fontSize violations in a 227-line file = 17.6 violations per 100 lines. For comparison, bookings-page is at 0.5/100 (UX-059). This is a conversion-critical page — Kira evaluates whether to upgrade to Pro — rendered with the worst visual discipline in the app. CheckIcon and XIcon functions at top (lines 7-12) inline-style the glyphs with fontSize 14 + fontWeight 700. FAQItem inline-styles everything. Should be class-driven throughout.
findings.suggested_fix: Pricing is a standalone marketing page with no shared components needs. Rewrite with tokens throughout. May be quicker than incremental fixes — candidate for Phase δ rewrite.

findings.title: payments-onboarding 43 fontSize violations in 952 lines; 10+ custom button components (PrimaryBtn, SecondaryBtn, etc.) reimplementing .btn
findings.description: src/pages/payments-onboarding-page.jsx defines PrimaryBtn (line 129), SecondaryBtn (line 166), FlowFrame (line 75), local StepDots (UX-060), plus AcknowledgeScreen, ResumeScreen, ActionRequiredScreen, RestrictedScreen (lines 372+). This is a mini-design-system inside one file, reimplementing components that exist in src/components/ui/. Any future design-system-level change (e.g. Phase δ's button modifier system) won't automatically flow to these local forks. And 43 fontSize violations even WITH those local components means the primitives themselves contain inline overrides. Likely the largest confidence-axis debt outside index.css itself.
findings.competitor_reference: Stripe Connect onboarding (uses the main design system throughout)
findings.measured_evidence: 10+ local button/screen components in a 952-line file
findings.suggested_fix: Refactor in Phase δ. Replace PrimaryBtn/SecondaryBtn with canonical .btn system. Extract AcknowledgeScreen etc. into a src/components/onboarding-flow/ folder as proper shared primitives — likely reusable by quote-builder's describe-phase too. Big job; schedule for Phase δ's final session.

findings.title: dashboard-page-v1.jsx win-close × button missing aria-label and uses glyph, not lucide
findings.description: v2-win-close at dashboard-page-v1.jsx:733 rendered a naked × glyph with font-size: 16px and no aria-label. Below the 44×44 touch target on mobile. Phase 6 flagged, Phase 7 fix.
findings.resolution: Phase 7 — imported lucide X, replaced glyph, added aria-label="Dismiss win card", flex-centered the button, added ::after tap expansion on (hover: none).

findings.title: quote-builder catalog overlay close ✕ missing aria-label, text-sized for glyph
findings.description: rq-catalog-close at quote-builder-page.jsx:1278 rendered ✕ with font-size: 13px and no aria-label. Mobile rule already enforced 44×44 but the icon was typographic, not vector. Phase 6 flagged, Phase 7 fix.
findings.resolution: Phase 7 — replaced ✕ with lucide X size=14, added aria-label="Close catalog", flex-centered the button in CSS.

findings.title: landing-page hero h2 uses inline color:'#fff' instead of a token
findings.description: landing-page.jsx:265 sets style={{color:'#fff'}} inline on an .lp-h2 element. The section has a permanent dark gradient background, so the visual result is correct, but it slipped past the Phase 4 hex-grep and is inconsistent with the --always-white token used elsewhere. No user-visible bug.

findings.title: analytics-page sparkline uses hardcoded #059669 instead of green token family
findings.description: analytics-page.jsx:113–122 renders the sparkline polyline, gradient stops, and data dots with hardcoded #059669 (Tailwind green-600). Dark-mode palette elsewhere uses --green-light (#4ade80) tints; the chart reads as dim green-600 against the dark surface. Not WCAG-failing, but inconsistent with the rest of the dark palette. Related to open UX-057 (chart palette) and should be folded into that fix.
findings.resolution: deferred to Session 1f — pairs with UX-057
